name: Benny Carter
description: American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, trumpeter, composer, arranger, and bandleader (1907-2003)
abstract: Bennett Lester Carter (August 8, 1907 – July 12, 2003) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, trumpeter, composer, arranger, and bandleader. With Johnny Hodges, he was a pioneer on the alto saxophone.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: I took J.J. Johnson’s place in Benny Carter’s orchestra, see? And I went out with Benny Carter and this was 1945... Benny Carter was a person that was tremendous help in teaching more how to read
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: I played drums with Benny, because he came through. It was wartime, and there weren’t very many drummers around. He hired me. I was with the band about four or five months
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: Even Benny Carter, Benny Carter and I were great friends, you know, and I think I was responsible for Benny Carter taking up trumpet. I gave him his first mouthpiece.

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Benny Carter — I did a gig with him called “Inside Benny Carter,” one of the greatest records he’s ever made.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: I've never met Benny Carter personally, but I've always admired him since I first heard him back in the Thirties.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerome Richardson
about_quotes.evidence: He started on the saxophone at an early age and was influenced by Johnny Hodges and Benny Carter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerome Richardson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996
